negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: International lane is RESTRICTED to Pathology and Pediatrics departments only — most other specialties are not open to international students. This is a critical caveat that any public-facing card must carry.
negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: Parkland Hospital is named as a placement site only on the U.S.-school page (not the international page); placement at Parkland for international students depends on which department accepts them.

nextAction: For public-safe activation: (a) SCHOOL_LEVEL_SOURCE — UTSW SOM hub; Parkland is the major clinical site; (b) international lane RESTRICTED to Pathology + Pediatrics ONLY — critical caveat; (c) Non-LCME US citizens at international MS are also accepted; (d) $150 app fee; (e) B-1 visa sponsored by UTSW; (f) max 8 weeks (two 4-wk); (g) 4-month lead time recommended.
plainEnglishSummary: Parkland Hospital is part of UT Southwestern's clinical site network. UTSW accepts visiting medical students through two tracks: US LCME schools via VSLO, and international medical schools via a paper/portal application. The international lane is RESTRICTED to two departments only — Pathology and Pediatrics. $150 non-refundable app fee. B-1 visa is sponsored by UTSW. Max two 4-week electives. 4-month lead time. Parkland is named as a placement site for several rotations.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because Texas gained its first P101 packet with an international-eligible USCE lane, but with the critical caveat that international access is limited to Pathology + Pediatrics — important to prevent overclaim that Parkland/UTSW is broadly open to international students.

userFacingSummaryDraft.oneSentenceSummary: UTSW (Parkland's academic affiliate) accepts international medical students RESTRICTED to Pathology and Pediatrics only; $150 nonrefundable app fee; B-1 visa sponsored by UTSW.
userFacingSummaryDraft.whoThisIsFor: International medical school students applying to UTSW's Pathology or Pediatrics departments; also non-LCME US citizens at international medical schools.
userFacingSummaryDraft.whoThisIsNotFor: International students in any specialty other than Pathology or Pediatrics; MD graduates; US LCME/COCA students (separate VSLO lane).
userFacingSummaryDraft.howToApply: UTSW institutional application; $150 non-refundable app fee; 4-month lead time recommended; UTSW International Office contacts on acceptance for B-1 visa.
userFacingSummaryDraft.estimatedCostSummary: $150 (USD) non-refundable application fee. Tuition not separately stated on international page.
keyCaveats: CRITICAL: International lane RESTRICTED to Pathology and Pediatrics only — most specialties closed
B-1 visa sponsored by UTSW (not student-arranged)
Also accepts US-citizen students at international medical schools
userFacingSummaryDraft.whyWeClassifiedItThisWay: INTERNATIONAL_STUDENT_CONFIRMED but with the strongest specialty restriction in P101 to date — only two UTSW departments accept international students.
userFacingSummaryDraft.possibleListingTitle: Parkland Health (UTSW) — International Visiting Students — Pathology & Pediatrics only (Dallas, TX)
userFacingSummaryDraft.possibleMetaDescription: UTSW/Parkland: international visiting MS RESTRICTED to Pathology and Pediatrics only; $150 app fee; B-1 visa sponsored by UTSW. Max 8 weeks.
